qa_pairs.question: What is a model checkpoint in TensorFlow?
qa_pairs.answer: A model checkpoint is a snapshot of your model at a given point during the training process, such as after a certain number of iterations or epochs, that is saved to disk and can be loaded at a later time.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it beneficial to create model checkpoints when training a TensorFlow model?
qa_pairs.answer: Creating model checkpoints is a good habit because it allows you to break long-running jobs into multiple shorter jobs that may be allocated more quickly on a cluster. It also helps prevent losing progress in case of unexpected errors in your code or node failures.

qa_pairs.question: How can you create a model checkpoint at the end of every training epoch using Keras in TensorFlow?
qa_pairs.answer: To create a checkpoint at the end of every training epoch when using Keras, you should utilize the `callbacks` parameter of the `model.fit()` method. An example of this is `tf.keras.callbacks.ModelCheckpoint(filepath="./ckpt", save_freq="epoch")`, ensuring the specified path for the checkpoint exists.
